name: Firstbank
description_short: FirstBank is a Tennessee-based community bank founded in 1906 with $16.3B in assets, offering personal and business banking services across 90 branch locations in the Southeast.
description_long:
FirstBank was founded in Tennessee in 1906 and is headquartered in Nashville. Over more than a century, the bank has grown into a substantial regional institution while maintaining a community banking focus. The bank now operates 90 full-service branch locations across the South and manages $16.3 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. FirstBank positions itself as a local community bank that emphasizes personal relationships and accessible banking rather than corporate bureaucracy.

FirstBank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking services. On the personal side, they provide checking and savings accounts (including their FirstUp Savings product with automatic transfers), certificates of deposit (CDs) currently earning 3.80% APY for 6 and 30-month terms, online banking, and mortgage services. For business customers, they offer business banking solutions, treasury management, trust services, and specialty lending. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. They provide customer support through a contact center (1-800-413-4211, available 7am-6pm CST Monday-Friday and 7am-4pm CST Saturday) and dedicated services for mortgages and manufactured homes lending.

FirstBank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on "Bank Local, Get More" — their core marketing philosophy that local banking provides more product options, more accessible bankers, and fewer corporate barriers. The bank has been recognized as a Top Workplace by the Tennessean for 11 consecutive years (as of 2025), suggesting strong employee satisfaction and organizational culture. Their growth over three decades while maintaining community banking principles and their expansion into South's major cities (Nashville, Memphis) demonstrates sustainable regional expansion without abandoning their local banking mission.

FirstBank is best suited for consumers and businesses in the Southeast seeking traditional community banking with personal relationships and FDIC protection. However, as a regional bank with 90 locations primarily in Tennessee and surrounding states, it offers limited geographic convenience for customers outside the South. Their CD rates (3.80% APY) are competitive but may not consistently rank as the absolute highest in the market. The website lacks detailed information about checking account features, overdraft policies, monthly fees, or digital banking capabilities, making full service comparison difficult. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (6-month and 30-month terms at 3.80% APY)
Manufactured homes customer service and lending
Business banking and small business accounts
Debit card services with lost/stolen card reporting
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Established 119-year history (founded 1906) with $16.3B in assets as of Dec 2025
90 full-service branch locations across the Southeast for in-person banking
Competitive CD rates at 3.80% APY for 6 and 30-month terms
FirstUp Savings account with automatic transfer convenience features
Dedicated local bankers available for personal and business relationships
Ranked Top Workplace in Tennessee for 11 consecutive years (2025)
cons: Limited geographic footprint — primarily Tennessee and surrounding states, not national coverage
Website lacks transparent fee schedules, overdraft policies, and detailed checking account feature comparisons
No information about mobile banking app capabilities, digital features, or ATM network size
CD rates (3.80% APY) are competitive but not consistently industry-leading on the website
Limited detail on business banking product specifics, loan terms, or small business lending criteria
best_for: Southeast consumers and small businesses seeking community banking with personal banker relationships
Customers in Tennessee, Nashville, Memphis, and surrounding Southern states wanting local branch access
Savers interested in competitive CD products with FDIC protection and established financial stability
Business owners preferring local banking relationships over national corporate institutions
diagnosis: FirstBank is ideal for Southeast-based consumers and businesses seeking community banking with FDIC protection, personal banker relationships, and local branch access. The main caveat is geographic limitation — the bank operates primarily in Tennessee and surrounding Southern states, making it unsuitable for national or non-Southern customers, and website transparency on specific fees, account features, and digital banking capabilities is limited.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
